based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted canonical bytes). Targets the SUM side of Rule 2 (vs the per-contribution side covered by mazur.bad-contribution.jsonl). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

what_was_NOT_mutated: backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.downstream_contributions[*] (both contributions left intact — sum(contributions) still equals the original sum)
backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value (-0.00877135469 — derived from the ORIGINAL sum; Rule 3 will cascade since stored signal_value no longer equals mutated sum * activation_derivative)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces at least one Rule 2 failure whose field_path names backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.backpropagated_sum. A Rule 3 cascade on h1.signal_value is expected and acceptable: signal_value was derived from the ORIGINAL sum, so when Rule 3 recomputes via the mutated sum, the stored signal_value disagrees.
cascading_failures_observed: Rule 3 on backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value: stored signal_value was derived from the original (unmutated) sum; recomputed = mutated_sum * activation_derivative differs by ~delta * activation_derivative ≈ 2.4e-7 (still > 1e-9 tolerance)
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage): proves the reconciler checks Rule 2's SUM half — backpropagated_sum == sum(stored contributions in summation_order). Distinct from mazur.bad-contribution.jsonl, which targets the per-contribution multiplicative check; together the two fixtures exercise both halves of Rule 2.
